title: neanotis urophylla
intro: Neanotis urophylla, also known as the 'Himalayan Blue Popppy', is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to alleviate fever, reduce pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Neanotis urophylla is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction by herbalists to be consumed as a tea, although its other uses and preparations may also vary.
